mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward instances where month-over-month RSAFS increased but S&P 500 20-day forward returns were negative in more than 55 percent of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25 percent, excess liquidity suppresses discount rates and risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered periods produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment combined with a zero-bound federal funds rate signals a dovish Fed put that limits equity downside, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 4 percent and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of occurrences or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production accelerates corporate operating leverage and earnings surprises during the rebound phase of the business cycle, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery periods identified by concurrent declining unemployment, month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of walk-forward instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals durable residential investment demand and spillover effects to cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is met, subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50 percent of occurrences or the average return is less than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month annualized inflation rate above 4 percent as measured by CPIAUCSL pressures real corporate margins and increases the probability of Fed tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences or the mean negative return is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above 2 percent annualized confirms aggregate demand momentum that translates into positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where GDPC1 exceeds 2 percent annualized, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances or the average return is not positive.
